info.title: REST API for Common Features in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications - Configuration Set Migration/Export
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.

Some REST resources have more than one version. In the Tasks section, when you expand a resource that has more than one version, you'll notice that the main folder contains subfolders, such as 11.13.18.05 and v1 (latest), corresponding to the available resource versions. Resources that don't have a newer version continue to appear without any change in their folder structure. You can find additional details on how to work with the different resource versions in the Quick Start and other topics in the Get Started section.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

post.description: Start a CSM archive export process.

requestBody.description: Information required to start CSM export process.

get.description: Check status of an export process.

202.description: Configuration Set status retrieved (in progress states).

name.description: Name of the configuration set to be exported.

description.description: Description of the configuration set to be exported.

isFullExport.description: Setting the flag to 'Y' will force a full migration instead of delta migration.

optionalModules.description: List of optional modules to be included in the exported configuration set.

SKIP_TARGET_CHECK.description: Setting the flag to `true` will skip release version comparison between source and registered target environment.

ExportOption.description: A (string, boolean) map of options. `SKIP_TARGET_CHECK` is an example key.

status.description: Short status code of the current operational status.

message.description: Description of the current operational state.

csId.description: The identifier of created Configuration Set.

userDetails.description: Instructions or hint for user to action upon.

targetPushStatus.description: Status of the configuration set archive being uploaded to target environment.

csmSandboxId.description: The identifier of the CSM Sandbox created on target environment as part of `Import` operation.

status.description: Short status code of the error.

userDetails.description: Instructions or hint for user to action upon.
